2606.06586.title: Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning
2606.06586.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) trained predominantly on English data encode substantial world knowledge, yet often fail to express it reliably in other languages, a phenomenon known as cross-lingual factual inconsistency. To study and address this, we introduce PolyFact, a large-scale parallel multilingual factual QA dataset containing 100K Wikidata-grounded facts across 12 typologically diverse languages. Using PolyFact, we compare light continual pretraining (CPT), supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinforcement learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for improving cross-lingual factual recall in Qwen-2.5-7B and OLMo-2-1124-7B. We find that GRPO consistently outperforms SFT, improving both cross-lingual consistency and generalization to unseen languages, while CPT on parallel data yields limited additional gains. Mechanistic analyses further show that GRPO reorganizes multilingual routing by reducing language specialization in MLP layers and attention heads, thereby promoting more shared cross-lingual representations. We release our code, models, and dataset.
2606.06586.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）主要基於英語數據訓練，編碼了大量的世界知識，然而在其他語言中卻常常無法可靠地表達這些知識，這一現象被稱為跨語言事實不一致。為了研究和解決這個問題，我們引入了PolyFact，一個大規模的平行多語言事實問答數據集，包含了12種類型多樣的語言中的10萬個基於Wikidata的事實。利用PolyFact，我們比較了輕量持續預訓練（CPT）、監督微調（SFT）和通過群體相對策略優化（GRPO）的強化學習，以提高Qwen-2.5-7B和OLMo-2-1124-7B中的跨語言事實回憶。我們發現GRPO在性能上始終優於SFT，改善了跨語言的一致性和對未見語言的泛化能力，而在平行數據上進行CPT則僅帶來有限的額外增益。機械分析進一步顯示，GRPO通過減少MLP層和注意力頭中的語言專門化來重新組織多語言路由，從而促進了更共享的跨語言表示。我們釋出了我們的代碼、模型和數據集。
2606.06586.author: Jonathan von Rad et.al.
2606.06586.authors: Jonathan von Rad, Louis Arts, George Burgess, Eleftheria Kolokytha, Harry O'Donnell, Ektor Oikonomidis Doumpas, Eduardo Sanchez, Yao Lu, Pontus Stenetorp
